refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, clarifying the performance requirement for teleoperation communication.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication link under defined network conditions using telemetry data and validate it meets ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to the operator for safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized low-latency communication protocols and data compression.
DP2: Implement edge processing to reduce data transmission volume.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic on the network link.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized low-latency communication protocols and data compression.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and vehicle control system timing.
Dependency on network link quality and safety services.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates latency reduction to communication protocol optimization, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with legacy vehicle systems.
Risk that compression may introduce processing delays.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge processing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing teleoperation communication protocols, data compression, and latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to provide latency and communication performance metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands from the NGHE Solution or operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not focused on communication protocol implementation.
Vehicle executes commands but does not manage communication latency directly.
Primary module responsible for communication latency management and protocol optimization.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency management is best contained within the Teleoperation module to centralize communication protocol logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces poorly with Vehicle or Network Link.
Risk of duplicated latency management logic if other modules implement overlapping functions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts and monitoring to avoid diffusion of latency logic across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by focusing on communication protocol optimization to meet latency requirements.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if latency logic is partially implemented in Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and is the logical owner of the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry interface and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency management to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface performance between Teleoperation and Vehicle to prevent latency degradation.
Review potential overlap with Safety Services for alerting on latency breaches.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the FR refinement can be further detailed to explicitly include reliability and feedback aspects, and the DP selection should consider fallback strategies if latency targets are not met.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to the operator for safe remote operation.

revise.why: To explicitly capture reliability and operator feedback requirements for safe teleoperation.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement required protocol optimizations.
open_risks: Latency impact from data compression processing time.
Potential diffusion of latency logic across modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under network conditions.
Interface performance metrics between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Verification of telemetry data accuracy for latency measurement.
